fault_hypothesis: Lines 71-72 incorrectly remove a duplicate last element when both lists end with the same value, but this element should be kept since both inputs contribute it. The pop() on line 72 is the bug.

constraints.intent: After the pop, merged should have exactly len(left)+len(right)-1 elements only if a true duplicate was removed — but this condition is always false when the pop is wrong.
The then-branch of the guard at line 71 should never be entered because removing a duplicate last element is always incorrect for a merge of two sorted lists.
After initialization, merged must be empty before any elements are appended.
The merged result must contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements since no elements should be dropped.
